key_insight: GeoSD improves reasoning generalization by making self-distillation respect the geometry of token probabilities, reducing harmful drift when the teacher’s privileged hints are hard for the student to justify.
engineering_relevance: For large language models used in math reasoning, GeoSD offers a practical post-training objective that preserves in-distribution gains while substantially improving OOD robustness.
method: Hellinger-weighted distillation attenuates teacher pressure on tokens the student cannot yet support.
A proximal Fisher-Rao regularizer keeps the student close to a recent checkpoint to limit accumulated drift.
Natural-gradient updates operate in the same distribution geometry as the loss terms.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning benchmarks and three model families, the method improves average OOD accuracy by 5.7–8.6 points over the base model, across scales from 1.7B to 32B.
watch_item: The approach adds geometric regularization and checkpoint-based proximity tracking, which may increase training complexity and sensitivity to hyperparameter tuning.
